model: deepseek-v4-pro sharded review with deepseek-v4-flash synthesis and deepseek-v4-pro focused follow-up
scope: LFG-P6.3 full browser-events migration to mounted Next.js App Router
summary: DeepSeek confirmed the full browser-events closeout now drives the mounted Next.js App Router UI and API rather than legacy desktop.html/mobile.html. The initial synthesis raised a P1 concern that OperationPanel might expose dangerous mutation controls on the default release page; a focused follow-up reviewed OperationPanel, page.tsx, operations.ts, and the frontend-acceptance output and confirmed the P1 was not real because OperationPanel renders controls only when workbench_event_controls=1 is present. A final closeout-delta review also passed after code-review coverage and P6.2-era test expectations were updated.

initial_synthesis_findings.summary: page.tsx renders OperationPanel and operations.ts exposes mutation helpers, so the default release page might expose dangerous controls.
initial_synthesis_findings.resolution: Focused follow-up confirmed OperationPanel starts disabled, reads URLSearchParams, returns null unless workbench_event_controls=1, and the release-default frontend acceptance output had no dangerous OperationPanel controls.

confirmed_behavior: tools/check-workbench-browser-events.mjs opens the mounted Next.js route with ?workbench_event_controls=1 and replays 15 browser-event closeout scenarios through the UI/API.
The browser-events artifact records route_family=nextjs_app_router, legacy_static_shell_used=false, legacy_interactions_replayed=true, and scenario_count=15.
OperationPanel mutation controls are not rendered on the default release page without the workbench_event_controls query parameter.
docs/governance/legacy-static-workbench-inventory.json and docs/governance/LARGE_FILE_GOVERNANCE_PLAN.md continue to block legacy static deletion on scheduler writeback and shell-test migration.

non_blocking_findings: Some evidence arrays and referenced review artifacts were outside the bounded review bundle.
The public browser route gate test references a script outside the bounded review bundle.
The remaining shell-test migration dependency is also tied to the large-file governance queue.
closeout_delta_review.summary: DeepSeek confirmed OperationPanel and operations.ts were added to the current code-review coverage denominator/shards, P6.3 retirement decision expectations are accurate, browser-events migration is no longer required_evidence_before_delete, and legacy static deletion remains blocked.
